title: Loki + Grafana + Promtail Infrastructure
description: Add Loki, Promtail, and Grafana services to docker-compose.prod.yml for production log aggregation. Loki (grafana/loki:3) stores logs with filesystem backend and 30-day retention, exposed only internally on port 3100. Promtail (grafana/promtail:3) scrapes Docker container logs via /var/lib/docker/containers, extracts labels (service name, environment), and parses JSON log lines to extract the log level. Grafana (grafana/grafana:11) provides the dashboard UI on port 3000 (localhost only). Create config files: monitoring/loki-config.yml, monitoring/promtail-config.yml. Auto-provision the Loki datasource in Grafana via monitoring/grafana/provisioning/datasources/loki.yml and dashboard loading via monitoring/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/dashboards.yml. Add named volumes for Loki and Grafana data persistence.
validation: Loki service added to docker-compose.prod.yml with grafana/loki:3 image
Promtail service added to docker-compose.prod.yml with grafana/promtail:3 image
Grafana service added to docker-compose.prod.yml with grafana/grafana:11 image
Loki port 3100 not exposed externally (internal network only)
monitoring/loki-config.yml exists with filesystem storage and 30-day retention
monitoring/promtail-config.yml exists and scrapes Docker container logs
Promtail pipeline parses JSON and extracts log level labels
Grafana Loki datasource auto-provisioned on startup
Grafana dashboard provider configured to load from provisioned directory
Named volumes for loki-data and grafana-data exist
All monitoring services have restart policies and health checks where applicable
